summary: User policy for the Recommended section in the Windows 11 Start menu. The local Microsoft StartMenu.admx file explicitly documents the same Explorer policy key, HideRecommendedSection value name, and Both-scope surface that the app uses for the current user.

setting.name: Start Menu Recommended Section (Current User)
setting.casual_explanation: Windows 11 Start can show a Recommended section with suggested apps and files. This user policy hides that section just for the current account.
setting.why_people_change_it: People hide it when they want a simpler Start menu or less visible recent-content surfacing without forcing the whole device.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Hiding the section makes Start cleaner for the current user, but it removes a built-in list of recommendations and recent items from that account's Start menu.
setting.professional_notes: This is the user-scoped variant of the Start policy. The CSP and StartMenu.admx document both device and user scope for HideRecommendedSection.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The current user is not forced to hide the Recommended section.

allowed_values.label: Recommended section shown
allowed_values.meaning: The section remains visible for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Recommended section hidden
allowed_values.meaning: The section is hidden for the current user.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes HideRecommendedSection = 1 under HKCU, which matches the documented hide behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 11 version 22H2 and later user profiles

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: Not configured corresponds to the ordinary Windows Start behavior for the current user.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want the normal Start experience
avoid_for: Users who want a cleaner Start menu for their own account
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the default Start menu behavior for your account.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to hide Recommended just for yourself.

recommended_profiles.label: Clean Start menu for current user
intended_for: Users who want a less suggestion-driven Start menu

states.rationale: Hide the Recommended section for the current user only.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to simplify your own Start menu without forcing the entire device.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You still want Start to surface recommended apps and files.
tradeoffs: Other users can keep their own Start configuration if no machine policy overrides it.

evidence.title: Microsoft Start Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Official documentation for HideRecommendedSection and HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites, including allowed values and scope.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft StartMenu.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps HideRecommendedSection and HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites to the Explorer policy key with both user and machine scope.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HideRecommendedSection = 1 under HKCU for this tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft StartMenu.admx file explicitly defines HideRecommendedSection under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer with class="Both", which means the same policy surface is valid for user scope as well as device scope. The official Start Policy CSP provides the 0/1 semantics, and the app writes that documented current-user hide value.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same Explorer policy key and HideRecommendedSection value used by the app, and marks the policy as class="Both", which supports both HKLM and HKCU policy scope. The official Start Policy CSP provides the 0/1 shown/hidden semantics for this same control.
